channel.title: Circuits on SoundsBrown
channel.description: Recent content in Circuits on SoundsBrown
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Optocouplers
item.description: Example circuits From the interwebs: Tested locally: 4N25 Test with circuit above suggests decay time of ~20ms after switching off. Input (J1) was square wave from LFO, output (J2) was to oscilloscope. Not great, but probably good enough for things like 1 PPS clock signals (200 BPM == 300 msec/beat). Note that https://hackaday.com/2018/05/09/optocouplers-defending-your-microcontroller-midi-and-a-hot-tip-for-speed/ suggests that adding some external circuitry can make the thing faster! H11L1 Should be equivalent to the PC900 mentioned in the MIDI spec.

item.title: Isolation
item.description: How do we make sure two interconnected modules don’t adversely affect one another? General Pass I/O connectors through diodes before they connect to the circuit. Use an opamp, see buffers. Digital signals Optocouplers. Dealing with differences in operating voltage Voltage dividers. Regulators.

item.title: Voltage dividers
item.description: If used with bipolar supply (e.g. +/- 12V), you divide the difference between + and -, and subtract that from + to get the output voltage: Vo = Vp - (Vp-Vn)*R1/(R1+R2) (where Vp is positive supply voltage, e.g. +12V and Vn is negative supply voltage, e.g. -12V)

item.title: Op amps as buffers / voltage followers
item.description: Circuit Op amps Pinouts Op amp Pinout LM741 TL072TL082 TL074TL084LM324 Application notes Supply voltage is important: All tested amps max out at somewhere between 3V and 4V when using a 5V supply. Use 12V and put other components (e.g. Arduino) behind a regulator! When using unipolar (0-12V) supply: LM741 floors at 2V or so – anything below that gets clamped.

item.title: RC filters and PWM
item.description: References https://www.electronics-tutorials.ws/filter/filter_2.html R and C value calculator: http://sim.okawa-denshi.jp/en/PWMtool.php About Arduino PWM outputs: https://playground.arduino.cc/Code/PwmFrequency/ https://www.arduino.cc/en/Tutorial/SecretsOfArduinoPWM Arduino PWM PWM base frequency: Pins 5 and 6: 62500 Hz Pins 3, 9, 10, and 11 is 31250 Hz Prescaler defaults to 64, resulting in default PWM rates of approximately 976 Hz and 488 Hz Changing the prescaler allows higher PWM rates, but may affect the behavior of micros(), delay(), et al.
